function.name: BLOCK
function.description: block/unblock phone apps + desktop websites only (NOT calendar time-blocks/focus-blocks — those go to CALENDAR create_event); actions block|unblock|status|request_permission|release|list_active; web requires confirmed:true

target.description: Which surface to act on: 'app' (phone apps) or 'website' (desktop hosts-file/SelfControl). Inferred from `action` (request_permission/release/list_active -> website), from app/website-only param shape, or from the user's text when omitted.

action.description: One of: block, unblock, status, request_permission, release, list_active. request_permission, release, and list_active are website-only.

intent.description: Free-form description of what the owner wants. Used by the block action to extract apps/hostnames + duration.

hostnames.description: (target=website) Public hostnames or URLs to block, e.g. ['x.com','twitter.com'].

confirmed.description: (target=website) Set true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, block returns a draft confirmation request. Required by release.

ruleId.description: (target=website, action=release) ID of the managed block rule to release.

reason.description: (target=website, action=release) Optional reason recorded on the rule when released.

includeLiveStatus.description: (target=website, action=list_active) Include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state. Default true.

includeManagedRules.description: (target=website, action=list_active) Include managed owner block rules. Default true.

packageNames.description: (target=app, Android) Package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android'].

appTokens.description: (target=app, iOS) iPhone app tokens from a previous selectApps() call.

durationMinutes.description: How long to block, in minutes. Omit/null for an indefinite block that stays active until manually removed.

confirmationRequired.description: Whether explicit confirmation is required before applying the block.

mode.description: Optional block mode such as harsh.

noBypass.description: Whether bypasses are disabled for the request.

schedule.description: Optional recurring schedule for the block.

exceptions.description: Optional exception windows for the block schedule.

scope.description: Optional status scope for block inspection.

includeScheduled.description: Whether scheduled block entries should be included in the response.

policy.description: Optional policy label for the block.

function.name: CALENDAR
function.description: calendar event CRUD + availability + prefs; subactions create_event|update_event|delete_event|search_events|propose_times|check_availability|next_event|feed

action.description: Which calendar operation to run. Calendar: feed, next_event, search_events, create_event, update_event, delete_event, trip_window, bulk_reschedule. Availability: check_availability, propose_times. Preferences: update_preferences.

intent.description: Natural-language description of the calendar request (e.g. "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm").

title.description: Event title, TOP-LEVEL flat field (NOT inside details). Example: { subaction: 'create_event', title: 'Dentist', details: { start, end } }

query.description: Search phrase for search_events / travel_itinerary (e.g. flight, dentist, Denver).

queries.description: Optional array of search phrases for search_events. Combined and deduped.

details.description: details (for create/update/delete_event ONLY): { calendarId, start (ISO-8601), end (ISO-8601), eventId, newTitle, location, attendees, description }. `title` is FLAT/TOP-LEVEL — never put it inside details. Time fields use `start`/`end` (aliases startAt/endAt). Example create_event: { subaction:'create_event', title:'Dentist', details:{ calendarId:'cal_primary', start:'2026-05-15T14:00:00Z', end:'2026-05-15T15:00:00Z', location:'Bright Smile Dental' } }. Example update_event: { subaction:'update_event', details:{ eventId:'event_00040', start:'...', end:'...' } }. For check_availability / propose_times / update_preferences, use TOP-LEVEL fields (startAt/endAt/durationMinutes/...), NOT details.

durationMinutes.description: Top-level flat field. Meeting length in minutes for propose_times. Example: `{ subaction: 'propose_times', durationMinutes: 30, slotCount: 3, windowStart: '...', windowEnd: '...' }`. Do NOT wrap propose_times args inside a `details` object.

daysAhead.description: Days ahead for propose_times search window (defaults to 7, ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied).

slotCount.description: Number of candidate slots for propose_times (defaults to 3).

windowStart.description: ISO-8601 earliest start of the propose_times search window.

windowEnd.description: ISO-8601 latest end of the propose_times search window.

startAt.description: Top-level flat field. ISO-8601 start time for check_availability. Example: `{ subaction: 'check_availability', startAt: '2026-05-14T09:00:00Z', endAt: '2026-05-14T10:00:00Z' }`. Do NOT wrap check_availability args inside a `details` object.

endAt.description: Top-level flat field. ISO-8601 end time for check_availability. See `startAt`.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for update_preferences (interprets preferred hours).

preferredStartLocal.description: Top-level flat field for update_preferences. Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h). Example: `{ subaction: 'update_preferences', preferredStartLocal: '09:00', preferredEndLocal: '17:00', blackoutWindows: [...] }`. Do NOT wrap update_preferences args inside a `details` object.

preferredEndLocal.description: Top-level flat field for update_preferences. Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h). See `preferredStartLocal`.

travelBufferMinutes.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

blackoutWindows.description: blackoutWindows[]: label startLocal HH:MM endLocal HH:MM daysOfWeek?[0..6]

durationMinutes.description: Top-level flat field. Meeting length in minutes for propose_times. Example: `{ subaction: 'propose_times', durationMinutes: 30, slotCount: 3, windowStart: '...', windowEnd: '...' }`. Do NOT wrap propose_times args inside a `details` object.

daysAhead.description: Days ahead for propose_times search window (defaults to 7, ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied).

slotCount.description: Number of candidate slots for propose_times (defaults to 3).

windowStart.description: ISO-8601 earliest start of the propose_times search window.

windowEnd.description: ISO-8601 latest end of the propose_times search window.

startAt.description: Top-level flat field. ISO-8601 start time for check_availability. Example: `{ subaction: 'check_availability', startAt: '2026-05-14T09:00:00Z', endAt: '2026-05-14T10:00:00Z' }`. Do NOT wrap check_availability args inside a `details` object.

endAt.description: Top-level flat field. ISO-8601 end time for check_availability. See `startAt`.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for update_preferences (interprets preferred hours).

preferredStartLocal.description: Top-level flat field for update_preferences. Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h). Example: `{ subaction: 'update_preferences', preferredStartLocal: '09:00', preferredEndLocal: '17:00', blackoutWindows: [...] }`. Do NOT wrap update_preferences args inside a `details` object.

preferredEndLocal.description: Top-level flat field for update_preferences. Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h). See `preferredStartLocal`.

travelBufferMinutes.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

blackoutWindows.description: blackoutWindows[]: label startLocal HH:MM endLocal HH:MM daysOfWeek?[0..6]

connector.description: Which connector to manage (kind from ConnectorRegistry, e.g. google, x, telegram, signal, discord, imessage, whatsapp, twilio, calendly, duffel, health, browser_bridge). Optional when subaction=list.

action.description: Lifecycle operation. connect (start auth/pairing); disconnect (revoke + clear grant); verify (active read/send probe where available). status/list are read-only diagnostics for explicit troubleshooting; prefer provider/core registry context when available. Strongly preferred - when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

mode.description: Connection mode: local | cloud_managed | remote. Defaults vary by connector.

recentLimit.description: verify only — how many recent messages/dialogs to read where the connector supports passive reads.

query.description: Discord verify only — optional search text to prove browser-message reads.

sendTarget.description: verify only — destination chat/recipient/channel for the self-test send.

sendMessage.description: verify only — text body for the self-test send.

profileId.description: browser_bridge connect only — profile identifier within the browser.

profileLabel.description: browser_bridge connect only — human-readable profile label.

redirectUrl.description: google/x connect only — optional OAuth redirect URL override.

phone.description: Optional phone number for the contact.

sourceEntityIds.description: Entity ids being folded into the target entity (merge). Provide as a JSON array of strings.

storeAllowed.description: Whether the benchmark may persist the identity or interaction update.

query.description: Required for search; free-form search string.

function.name: LIFE_COMPLETE
function.description: Complete a target, usually a reminder. Include target.

function.name: LIFE_CREATE
function.description: Create personal life records such as reminders, alarms, workouts, or health metrics. Use subaction=create and put typed fields in details.

function.name: LIFE_REVIEW
function.description: Review life records without mutating state.

target.description: Optional target record or entity being skipped.

reason.description: Reason recorded for the skip.

function.name: LIFE_SNOOZE
function.description: Snooze a reminder-like target. Include target and minutes.

target.description: Optional target record or entity being updated.

updates.description: Partial updates to the benchmark record.

function.name: MESSAGE
function.description: primary message action send read_channel read_with_contact search list_channels list_servers join leave react edit delete pin get_user triage list_inbox search_inbox draft_reply draft_followup respond send_draft schedule_draft_send manage dm group channel room thread user server inbox draft

action.description: Message action. One of: send, read_channel, read_with_contact, search, list_channels, list_servers, join, leave, react, edit, delete, pin, get_user, triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft_reply, draft_followup, respond, send_draft, schedule_draft_send, manage.

source.description: Connector source such as discord, slack, signal, whatsapp, telegram, x, imessage, matrix, line, google-chat, feishu, instagram, wechat, gmail.

accountId.description: Optional connector account id for multi-account message connectors.

sources.description: Optional inbox sources for op=triage, list_inbox, or search_inbox.

folder.description: Optional inbox folder hint for triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft, or respond operations.

target.description: Loose target reference: user, handle, channel, room, group, server, contact, phone, email, or platform-specific ID.

targetKind.description: Target kind for op=send: user, contact, channel, room, thread, group, server, email, or phone.

channel.description: Channel/room/group reference for read_channel, list_channels, join, leave.

roomId.description: Platform room or stored room ID.

server.description: Loose server, guild, workspace, or team name/reference.

serverId.description: Platform server, guild, workspace, or team ID.

userId.description: Platform user ID or stored entity ID.

entityId.description: Person/entity ID for read_with_contact, get_user, or scoped search.

platform.description: Optional platform filter for read_with_contact or search.

thread.description: Optional thread parent reference for op=send.

alias.description: Channel/room alias for op=join or op=leave.

invite.description: Invite URL or token for op=join.

message.description: Message text for op=send or replacement text for op=edit.

text.description: Replacement text for op=edit (alias of message).

query.description: Search term for op=search or op=search_inbox.

content.description: Inbox search text or message lookup hint for triage/draft/respond.

sender.description: Sender identifier for inbox search or reply lookup.

body.description: Draft or response body for draft_reply, draft_followup, respond.

reply.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

replyText.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

messageBody.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

draftId.description: Draft identifier for op=send_draft or op=schedule_draft_send.

confirmed.description: Whether the user explicitly confirmed sending for op=send_draft.

messageId.description: Platform/full message ID or stored memory ID for react/edit/delete/pin/respond.

inReplyToId.description: Alias for messageId when drafting or responding to a message.

id.description: Alias for messageId, accepted for planner compatibility.

pin.description: Pin state for op=pin (false to unpin when supported).

manageOperation.description: Management operation for op=manage: archive, trash, spam, mark_read, label_add, label_remove, tag_add, tag_remove, mute_thread, unsubscribe.

label.description: Label for op=manage when adding/removing labels.

tag.description: Tag for op=manage when adding/removing tags.

urgency.description: Urgency for op=send: normal, important, urgent.

persist.description: Whether op=send persists outbound content to target room memory. Defaults true.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.

worldIds.description: Optional account/server scopes for inbox operations.

channelIds.description: Optional channel/conversation scopes for inbox operations.

until.description: End date/timestamp for op=read_channel range=dates or op=search_inbox.

groupBy.description: Optional grouping key for the spending summary.

category.description: Optional category filter for the subscription audit.

function.name: MONEY_SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL
function.description: Cancel a subscription. Include confirmed=true only when the user has authorized cancellation.

candidateId.description: Optional benchmark-only candidate identifier for the cancellation flow.

kind.description: Optional override. Defaults to the owner surface's backing kind.

intent.description: Free-form owner request used for extraction and replies.

title.description: Item title — TOP-LEVEL (flat) field. NEVER place title inside `details`. Example: { action: 'create', kind: 'definition', title: 'Pick up uniforms', details: { kind: 'reminder', due: '2026-05-11T09:00:00Z', listId: 'list_personal' } }.

intent.description: Free-form owner request used for extraction and replies.

title.description: Item title — TOP-LEVEL (flat) field. NEVER place title inside `details`. Example: { action: 'create', kind: 'definition', title: 'Pick up uniforms', details: { kind: 'reminder', due: '2026-05-11T09:00:00Z', listId: 'list_personal' } }.

target.description: Existing item id or title for update/delete/complete/skip/snooze/review.

details.description: Structured schedule/cadence/notes — put `due` (ISO-8601 timestamp), `listId`, `kind` (reminder|alarm|habit|routine|todo), `cadence`, `description`, `metadata` HERE inside `details`. `title` is FLAT/TOP-LEVEL — NEVER nest it here. Example create-a-reminder: { action:'create', kind:'definition', title:'Pick up uniforms', details:{ kind:'reminder', due:'2026-05-11T09:00:00Z', listId:'list_personal' } }.

function.name: RESOLVE_REQUEST

kind.description: ScheduledTaskKind for create + filter for list. One of reminder, checkin, followup, approval, recap, watcher, output, custom.

status.description: Status filter for list (string or string[]). One of scheduled, fired, acknowledged, completed, skipped, expired, failed, dismissed.

subjectKind.description: ScheduledTaskSubject.kind: entity | relationship | thread | document | calendar_event | self.

ownerVisibleOnly.description: When true, list returns only ownerVisible tasks.

promptInstructions.description: create-only: prompt instructions stored on the task.

trigger.description: create-only: ScheduledTaskTrigger object (once / cron / interval / relative_to_anchor / during_window / event / manual / after_task).

contextRequest.description: create-only: structured context request for owner facts, entities, relationships, recent task states, or event payload.

shouldFire.description: create-only: structural gate composition; use gate refs rather than prompt text conditions.

completionCheck.description: create-only: structural completion check such as user_replied_within, user_acknowledged, subject_updated, or health_signal_observed.

output.description: create-only: output destination/target, e.g. { destination: 'channel', target: 'in_app:<roomId>' }.

pipeline.description: create-only: child ScheduledTask refs for onComplete/onSkip/onFail.

escalation.description: create-only: escalation ladder or explicit channel steps.

idempotencyKey.description: create-only: stable key for deduping repeated schedules.

respectsGlobalPause.description: create-only: when true, the task skips during global pause.

ownerVisible.description: create-only: when true, the task surfaces in owner views.

source.description: create-only: task source (default_pack | user_chat | first_run | plugin).

minutes.description: snooze-only: minutes to defer next fire.

untilIso.description: snooze-only: ISO-8601 timestamp to defer next fire to.

reason.description: skip / complete / acknowledge / dismiss / reopen: free-form reason recorded on the state log.

patch.description: update-only: shallow patch of editable ScheduledTask fields.

sinceIso.description: history-only: ISO-8601 lower bound on log occurredAtIso.

includeRollups.description: history-only: include rolled-up daily summary log rows (default false; raw rows only).

function.name: SCHEDULED_TASK_CREATE
function.description: Create a scheduled task. Include kind, trigger, promptInstructions, and other structured task fields when known.

output.description: Optional task output routing or payload descriptor.

subject.description: Optional subject entity or thread anchor for the scheduled task.

pipeline.description: Optional pipeline metadata for the benchmark task record.

respectsGlobalPause.description: Whether the task should respect a global pause flag.

metadata.description: Benchmark-only metadata for scenario provenance and variant tracking.

escalation.description: Optional escalation ladder for multi-channel follow-up.

updates.description: Partial task field updates to apply.

action.description: Action: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

todos.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for action=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

includeCompleted.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in action=list output.

limit.description: Max rows to return for action=list.

function.name: VOICE_CALL
function.description: Twilio voice dial: recipientKind=owner|external|e164; draft-confirm; approval-queue

action.description: Single canonical verb: `dial`.

recipientKind.description: Recipient discriminator: `owner` (escalation; uses owner number env), `external` (third party; recipient name resolved via relationships, allow-list checked), or `e164` (raw E.164 phone in `phoneNumber`).

phoneNumber.description: For recipientKind=e164: destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).

recipient.description: For recipientKind=external: contact name or E.164 phone number. Names resolve via the relationships store.

bodyText.description: Optional spoken message played when the call connects.

confirmed.description: Must be true to actually place the call. Without it the action returns a draft / approval-queue entry.

reason.description: Optional reason describing why the call is being placed (recorded with the approval task).
